name: Tony (Tongyun) Yang
tagline: mean the limits of the world?
in more than we can name.
openingHeadline: Built across LLMs, vision, world models, and medical AI. Now I want to go deeper, with passion and faith.
openingBody: I've shipped research with Tencent, Gradient Networks, MeetaVista, TU Delft, McGill, Tsinghua, and IMDEA. The throughline I care about: useful, durable AI, especially where access and reliability matter.
currently: An independent AI researcher in Amsterdam. I currently collaborate with industry (Tencent, Gradient Networks, MeetaVista) and academia (TU Delft, McGill, Tsinghua) on cost-efficient large language models, optimization-as-reasoning, and intent-aware world models. Earlier roles include Marie Skłodowska-Curie Fellow, AI Research Engineer at TU Delft Imaging Physics, and an MSc degree from TU Delft.
intro: Open to research, in academia or industry. What pulls me is meaning more than topic.
researchThemesIntro: Two things drive this work: pushing AI beyond text into perception and action, and making those systems fairer and more reachable in practice.

researchThemes.title: World models
researchThemes.blurb: Systems that perceive a scene and imagine what happens next. The bridge from describing the world to acting in it.

researchThemes.title: Large language models
researchThemes.blurb: Making capable models cheap, dependable, and useful enough to actually deploy. Routing, optimization, and agent design.

researchThemes.title: Computer vision
researchThemes.blurb: Vision as a channel for human signal, not just object detection. Reading emotion, intent, and attention from what people see.

researchThemes.title: AI for medicine
researchThemes.blurb: Models that work in the real clinic, not only on the leaderboard. Smaller, faster, and fair across patient populations.

researchThemes.title: On-device models
researchThemes.blurb: Perception and language models that run locally, not in the cloud. Distillation, quantization, and the craft of keeping small systems capable.

researchThemes.title: Social & economic behavior
researchThemes.blurb: How crowds price information, shift positions, and reveal preferences over time. Reading collective signal from messy human data.

researchThemes.title: Human-computer interaction
researchThemes.blurb: Where AI stops being a model and becomes a tool. Designing systems that fit how people actually think, type, and decide.

researchThemes.title: Fairness × access
researchThemes.blurb: A motivation, not a sub-field. Anything I build should reach the people who need it most, not the people who already have everything.

researchDek: What runs through every paper here. I want this work to be useful where it matters, accessible to people who don't already have it, and meaningful enough to keep at for years.
projectsIntro: Where the research meets real systems. Some are live collaborations (Gradient Networks, Tencent, MeetaVista); others I build on my own time. Most are still in motion.

contactInvitation: I read everything. Reach out about research, collaboration, supervision, or just to say hi. I answer faster than is professional, and I bring my whole self to the conversation.
